title: johnstonella gypsites
intro: Johnstonella gypsites, also known as gypsophila gypsophila, is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, and is often found growing in dry, sandy areas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of Johnstonella gypsites include teas and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including respiratory problems and skin irritations.
